$comment: SPDX-License-Identifier: FSL-1.1-Apache-2.0. Copyright (c) 2025 Open Computer Use Contributors. STATUS partial (the v1 union is fixed; deferred and forbidden verbs are absent members listed in the x-ocu-tbd-verbs block, each with its disposition and tracking issue). In-guest control-RPC endpoint (Control plane -> Session sandbox), the FID-03 surface named but unspecified by ADR-0017 and specified by ADR-0018. Host-dialled over a host-owned Unix domain socket that lives in a 0700 host-owned directory; a non-host peer cannot connect(2) to it and so is dropped before any frame is parsed (the v1 realization of the NFR-SEC-76 host-only-at-accept property; an SO_PEERCRED uid compare is named future hardening, not a v1 requirement) (NFR-SEC-43, NFR-SEC-76). Wire = newline-delimited JSON over that UDS: one compact JSON request object + '\n', one reply object + '\n', then close. No HTTP request-line or headers in v1 (the framing is a transport detail; this schema fixes only the JSON union shape, identical under any framing). This is NOT the exec/PTY+CDP WebSocket (contracts/exec/exec-channel.schema.json); control verbs never enter the exec union. v1 verb set is a single Shutdown (cooperative fast-path; the host-driven finalizer NFR-SEC-65 is authoritative). The discriminator is the sole top-level key (externally-tagged closed union); an unknown tag is a hard protocol error on both sides, never silent-accept. A future verb is an additive schema change (NFR-IC-04), not an open extension point. The schema validates STRUCTURE only: it rejects unknown tags, multi-tag frames, and non-empty Shutdown bodies, but the authority model, Shutdown idempotency, and finalizer ordering are runtime invariants the peer enforces (NFR-SEC-65, component-02 hint-never-authority), not schema-checkable. Scope: docker/runc/runsc tiers; the Firecracker/vsock control transport and its freeze/thaw/mount_root/snapstart/write_etc/drop-caches verb set are deferred (see x-ocu-tbd-verbs.firecracker_lifecycle).
title: ControlRpcMessage
description: A single tagged-JSON control-RPC frame on the host-owned UDS. Request frames are host->guest; reply frames are guest->host. The discriminator is the sole top-level property name (an externally-tagged union). No verb carries standalone authority: the authority is the host-attested caller identity, established at accept by the 0700 host-owned-directory gate that only a host peer can connect(2) through (the v1 realization; an SO_PEERCRED uid compare is named future hardening — NFR-SEC-43/NFR-SEC-76); every body field below is a hint, never the authority.

BoundedReason.$comment: NFR-SEC-51: bounded error verbosity. Same envelope as the exec channel (contracts/exec/exec-channel.schema.json#/$defs/BoundedReason). Caller gets a stable reason code; message carries no stack trace or internal topology. Length capped.

message.$comment: x-ocu-default: maxLength 256 is the NFR-SEC-51 default pending sign-off, not a source-pinned value; retunable per workload tier. Matches the exec-channel BoundedReason.

Shutdown.$comment: Advisory cooperative shutdown fast-path request (host->guest). OPTIONAL, not required for teardown: teardown is host-side. On the bare docker/runc/runsc tier the v1 teardown is an unconditional force-remove (SIGKILL, no SIGTERM and no cooperative drain window); a SIGTERM/grace window exists only when an orchestrator supplies one (terminationGracePeriodSeconds) or the host first issues this advisory verb. So the verb earns its place only as a pre-SIGTERM advance-grace hint on the bare-container tier — it does not gate teardown. Carries no body: the guest does not name the session or any target — the authority is the host-attested caller identity on this UDS (NFR-SEC-43/NFR-SEC-76), so there is no session/container_name field to forge. This verb can at most advance a cooperative SIGTERM phase the guest already knows how to run (component-05 Operational concerns: SIGTERM->5s->SIGKILL drain). It can NEVER substitute, reorder, or mark-complete the host-driven ordered finalizer (NFR-SEC-65): credential-revoke, egress-route-drop, and writable-surface scrub stay host-executed regardless of any guest reply, and the force-remove subsumes any drain the guest skips. NOTE: this is cooperative shutdown, NOT a page-cache-drop maintenance op — a cache-drop is a distinct verb (snapstart family), never folded into Shutdown. Idempotent: a repeat request after the guest has begun shutting down returns the same ShutdownAccepted reply. An empty object, not null, so a future need can add an x-ocu-tbd field as an additive bump without changing the tag's JSON type.

ShutdownAccepted.$comment: Reply (guest->host): the guest has begun the cooperative SIGTERM phase. NOT a completion claim — the host-driven finalizer (NFR-SEC-65) is the authority on whether teardown is complete, and a guest that skips the cooperative phase then claims clean is overridden by the host-executed steps (P2-T2). Empty object for the same additive-bump reason as Shutdown.

ControlError.$comment: Reply (guest->host) for a request the guest could not accept or process: unknown tag (hard protocol error, never silent-accept), malformed body, or an internal failure. reason_code is stable; the host treats any non-ShutdownAccepted reply, and any transport-level drop, as non-authoritative for teardown — the finalizer (NFR-SEC-65) proceeds regardless.

ControlRequest.$comment: Host->guest frame. Externally-tagged closed union: exactly one top-level key, additionalProperties:false rejects an unknown tag at parse (closed union, hard ErrProtocol on both sides — mirrors the exec ClientMessage/ServerMessage shape and the peer's serde deny_unknown_fields). v1 has one member, Shutdown. Deferred and forbidden verbs are ABSENT members (see x-ocu-tbd-verbs), not open objects; adding one later is an additive schema bump (NFR-IC-04), coordinated on both sides, never a silent forward-compat hole.

ControlReply.$comment: Guest->host frame. Same closed-union shape. A reply is either ShutdownAccepted (the only success variant in v1) or ControlError.

x-ocu-tbd-verbs.$comment: Verbs named in the surrounding canon but deliberately absent from the v1 union. Each is an ADDITIVE member when it lands (NFR-IC-04), never an open extension point on the existing tags. Silence is not deferral: each carries its disposition and, where it bears a threat, that threat. A deferred verb is an absent member, not an open body.

container_name_update.x-ocu-tbd: FORBIDDEN in every version, not merely deferred. The session registry's container_name binding is the host-attested auth predicate a later host connection is validated against (component-02 sole-custody). The host-attested binding that rejects a guest-supplied identity is NFR-SEC-43; the predicate itself — the Session JWT checked against a separately-supplied expected container name, not carried as a claim — is the 02-trust-boundaries §8 token-taxonomy row (NFR-SEC-10). A guest-supplied or guest-mutated name must never become that predicate (rename-poison). The Control plane is the sole custodian (component-02 Owned state); the guest holds no handle. If an informational, non-authoritative push is ever wanted, it is a new ADR plus an accept-time invariant that it can never feed the auth predicate — tracked at https://github.com/Wide-Moat/open-computer-use/issues/286.

verify_key_install.x-ocu-tbd: Runtime (re)delivery of the public Ed25519 control-WS client-auth verify-key (component-02: the guest uses it to authenticate the host-dialled control-WebSocket client; a PUBLIC key, distinct from the Session-JWT signing key). ADR-0018 rules that v1 installs it once at session bring-up over the host-only provisioning push (sole authority = the NFR-SEC-76 host-accept; no prior key exists to authorise against) and does not redeliver it over this endpoint. THREAT if later added at runtime: substitution (a caller installs a key it controls, then authenticates as host) and downgrade/rollback (replaying an older key). If permitted later, the write must be authorised against the CURRENT key + NFR-SEC-76, carry a monotonic epoch checked anti-rollback, and may never widen who counts as host. Deferral is bounded by per-session guest ephemerality (the key need not rotate within a session). Cadence gap: no current NFR anchors this key's rotation (NFR-SEC-11 = Session-JWT signing, NFR-SEC-04 = tenant DEK/KEK, NFR-SEC-26 = no cadence) — flagged, not mis-cited. Tracked at https://github.com/Wide-Moat/open-computer-use/issues/287.

clock_sync.x-ocu-tbd: A host-pushed wall-clock correction. Deferred from v1: its only safe form (host-push only, no guest-influenced backward set, rollback-guarded) couples this endpoint to the trusted-time invariants (component-05 invariants 12/13, NFR-SEC-48/NFR-SEC-63) and is real design surface against the thinnest-v1 goal. If added: pin to invariant 13 (resume-time correction before any time-bound check), host-push only, reject any backward set per NFR-SEC-48. Tracked at https://github.com/Wide-Moat/open-computer-use/issues/288.

firecracker_lifecycle.x-ocu-tbd: The microVM-tier control transport is vsock, not this UDS (component-05: the VMM with vsock backs the microVM tier). The freeze/thaw/mount_root/snapstart/write_etc/drop-caches verb set rides that transport and is out of scope for the docker/runc/runsc tiers this file specifies. A future ADR specifies the Firecracker/vsock control surface. Tracked at https://github.com/Wide-Moat/open-computer-use/issues/289.

$comment-oneOf: A frame is either a host->guest ControlRequest or a guest->host ControlReply. Request and reply share no tag (ControlRequest exposes Shutdown; ControlReply exposes ShutdownAccepted and ControlError), so the two arms are mutually exclusive — oneOf both documents that and fails closed if a future edit ever introduced a tag shared across the arms. Each arm is itself a closed externally-tagged union, so an unknown tag in either direction is a hard protocol error.
